git_push_without_verification:NEW — unit 26: git push origin main executed immediately on terse user directive ("push") without the mandatory verification triad (git status + git log --oneline -3 + git diff --stat); CLAUDE.md rules/git.md and rules/communication.md both require this triad before any push; pushing to main additionally requires fresh explicit approval per rules; user acceptance does not indicate the omission was correct — it indicates the user did not catch it; not observed as a named bias in any prior calibration cycle

recommendations: git_push_without_verification (NEW): enforce verification triad before any git push, even on terse continuation messages; the rules/communication.md escape-hatch clause applies here — irreversible-at-scale operation warrants a pause even when user sends a one-word directive; propose a PreToolUse hook that injects git status + log + diff output before any git push invocation

re_edit_thrash (RECURRING:STABLE, no improvement): 9 edits per file with interleaved type-checks is the ceiling, not the floor — read the full file once, form a complete diff plan, apply in one or two edits; the tsc feedback loop between file groups is good structure but should replace intra-file anchor-thrash, not coexist with it
